title: A Speedy New Way to Start Races
story: Runners, take your marks! In 1888, Charles Sherrill of Yale University changed the game of track and field forever. On May 12, he introduced a revolutionary way to begin races: the crouching start. Instead of standing up, athletes now got down low, ready to spring forward like cats. This clever technique helped runners blast off the starting line faster than ever before!
activity_question: What new technique did Charles Sherrill introduce?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the crouching start helps runners go faster?

title: The Voice in the Air
story: Voices traveling through thin air? In 1908, Nathan B. Stubblefield made it possible. On May 12, he patented wireless radio broadcasting, paving the way for a revolution in communication. His invention allowed sounds to be sent over long distances without wires, bringing news and entertainment to people's homes in ways never before imagined.
activity_question: What did Nathan B. Stubblefield patent?
follow_up_question: How do you think radio changed people's lives back then?

title: Guardians of the Sky: NORAD Takes Flight
story: High above the clouds, a new team of sky watchers formed in 1958. The United States and Canada joined forces to create NORAD - the North American Air Defense Command. On May 12, these friendly neighbors agreed to work together, keeping an eye on the skies to protect both countries. It was like having a giant protective shield over North America!
activity_question: What does NORAD stand for?
bonus_fact: NORAD tracks Santa Claus every Christmas Eve, delighting children across North America.

title: Stormy Weather Gets a Gender Makeover
story: Hurricanes were getting a new identity! In 1978, the US Commerce Department announced a big change. No longer would these powerful storms be given only female names. This decision on May 12 meant future hurricanes would have both male and female names, making the naming system more balanced and fair.
activity_question: What change did the US Commerce Department announce?
activity_choices: Hurricanes would have both male and female names
Hurricanes would only have male names
Hurricanes would be named after animals
Hurricanes would have no names at all
activity_answer: Hurricanes would have both male and female names
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to use both male and female names for hurricanes?

title: Brazil Beats the Zika Buzz
story: Mosquitoes met their match in 2017! Brazil announced some buzzing good news on May 12. The country declared an end to its Zika emergency as cases of the mosquito-borne illness dropped. Health officials worked hard to stop the pesky insects from spreading the virus. Their efforts paid off, making Brazil safer for everyone.
activity_question: What did Brazil declare an end to in 2017?
follow_up_question: How do you think people in Brazil felt when they heard this news?
